title: Mark 3:22-30 reads.  “And the teachers of the law who came down from Jerusalem said, “He is possessed by Beelzebul! By the prince of demons he is driving out demons.” So Jesus called them over to him and began to speak to them in parables:  “How can Satan drive out Satan? If a kingdom is divided against itself, that kingdom cannot stand. If a house is divided against itself, that house cannot stand. And if Satan opposes himself and is divided, he cannot stand; his end has come. In fact, no one can enter a strong man’s house without first tying him up. Then he can plunder the strong man’s house. Truly I tell you, people can be forgiven all their sins and every slander they utter, but whoever blasphemes against the Holy Spirit will never be forgiven; they are guilty of an eternal sin.” He said this because they were saying, “He has an impure spirit.”  These were terrible allegations.
scripture_ref: “Then Jesus entered a house, and again a crowd gathered, so that he and his disciples were not even able to eat. Then his family heard about this, they went to take charge of him, for they said, “He is out of his mind.” Then Jesus’ mother and brothers arrived. Standing outside, they sent someone in to call him. A crowd was sitting around him, and they told him, “Your mother and brothers are outside looking for you.” “Who are my mother and my brothers?” he asked. Then he looked at those seated in a circle around him and said “Here are my mother and my brothers! Whoever does God’s will is my brother and sister and mother.”
scripture_text: We want to look at the family of Jesus, and how they related to him, and how He related to them. Mark tells us of two important pieces of information about Jesus' family, divided by a very painful and terrible incident in the life of Jesus.
devotional:
Some Historical background about the family of Jesus.

Jesus had at least 6 siblings, two or more were sisters and four brothers -James, Joses, Judas and Simon (Mark 6.3). James became the head of the early church in Jerusalem after the crucifixion and resurrection of Jesus. Mary, His mother was probably married at 12 or 13 years of age and would have been in her early 40's at the time of Jesus' violent death outside Jerusalem.

Conflict within his family.

What Mark says here is very interesting and can teach us many lessons.

Jesus is not only facing opposition from the religious authorities, but he is also facing pressure from his family. His family wanted to control him. 'When his family heard about this, they went to take charge of him for they said, 'he is out of his mind."

Jesus' family thought he was "out of his mind." The idea behind the phrase for 'out of his mind' means to be out of position. They thought that Jesus had lost his way, and they may have felt embarrassed by him. Nazareth was about 30 miles from Capernaum so why did Jesus' family make such a long trip to seek Him out? Because they wanted to control him. The word used for 'Take charge of' is the Greek word Krateo. This is a very strong word, which means to seize or arrest. We are not told precisely why they took this very strong decision. It is possible that when His family heard the things being reported concerning what he was doing, they decided that Jesus must have lost contact with reality and so they rushed out to locate Him to reduce the shame of the both the family and the family name.

The arrival at the house.

When Jesus' family leave their home and arrive at the house where Jesus was staying. They sent someone in to tell him that they, his family wanted him outside. 'Who are my mother and brothers?' He asked. Then he looked at those seated in a circle around him and said. 'Here are my mother and brothers! Whoever does God's will is my brother and sister and mother." In Matthew's gospel we read "He replied to him. Pointing to his disciples, he said, 'Here are my mother and my brothers. For whosoever does the will of my Father in heaven is my Brother and sister and brother."

To help us understand what Jesus meant when he said these words, we must keep in mind the whole context of this incident. Jesus was under great pressure, he had been accused of being demonised, even His family thought he was crazy, so he must have been feeling lonely and vulnerable.

The crowds around Jesus would have believed because of the importance of the family in that culture that family members have priority relationship over others. But instead of dropping everything and going out to see his family, Jesus gives a response that would have shocked all that were listening.

There are a number of lessons we need to learn from Jesus' response.

1. Jesus draws a contrast between his natural family and his Spiritual family. There are two ways we can relate to Jesus, we either relate to him naturally or spiritually. When we relate to Jesus at a natural level, we will be like his family, we will try and control him, we will expect him to come to us, our communication with him will be all about us trying to control and manipulate him. On the other hand, to relate to Jesus spiritually is to have a submissive heart that bows to the will of his Father.

2. Enjoying God as father and doing his will increases a persons capacity to relate to Jesus. We cannot live in devotion to the Father and not have intimacy with Jesus.

3.The family of God is made up of those who live under the same authority as Jesus Christ. We cannot be committed to Jesus until we are as committed as Jesus is.

4.Jesus finds his fellowship and acceptance with those who do the will of his father. At this vulnerable time in Jesus' life, he needed those who would understand and accept him. Only those who have a heart set on doing the Fathers will can accept Jesus and what he stands for.

As Jesus pointed out to those seated around him, He was expressing his hearts desire for real intimacy with those who are called disciples. The attitude of Jesus' family towards him at this time should cause us to ask some very important questions.

Why do people try to control others?

What does the controls of others look like? Mark 3:20-21 has a lot to teach us about how as people, we can fall into the trap of trying to control others, or even try to control God. When we set out to control others it is always a deliberate wilful choice we make. It is never a spontaneous thing; it is not the result of a character defect. But the result of deliberate responsible choice. Jesus’ family "went to take charge of him."
prayer: Prayer, Father help me understand and accept the members of my family as they are, even when they do and think differently to me.

By the prince of demons he is driving out demons.” So Jesus called them over to him and began to speak to them in parables: “How can Satan drive out Satan? If a kingdom is divided against itself, that kingdom cannot stand. If a house is divided against itself, that house cannot stand. And if Satan opposes himself and is divided, he cannot stand; his end has come. In fact, no one can enter a strong man’s house without first tying him up. Then he can plunder the strong man’s house. Truly I tell you, people can be forgiven all their sins and every slander they utter, but whoever blasphemes against the Holy Spirit will never be forgiven; they are guilty of an eternal sin.” He said this because they were saying, “He has an impure spirit.” These were terrible allegations.

“Then Jesus entered a house, and again a crowd gathered, so that he and his disciples were not even able to eat. Then his family heard about this, they went to take charge of him, for they said, “He is out of his mind.” Then Jesus’ mother and brothers arrived. Standing outside, they sent someone in to call him. A crowd was sitting around him, and they told him, “Your mother and brothers are outside looking for you.” “Who are my mother and my brothers?” he asked. Then he looked at those seated in a circle around him and said “Here are my mother and my brothers! Whoever does God’s will is my brother and sister and mother.”

Some Historical background about the family of Jesus.

What Mark says here is very interesting and can teach us many lessons.

Jesus' family thought he was "out of his mind." The idea behind the phrase for 'out of his mind' means to be out of position. When Jesus' family leave their home and arrive at the house where Jesus was staying. They sent someone in to tell him that they, his family wanted him outside. 'Who are my mother and brothers?' He asked. Then he looked at those seated in a circle around him and said. 'Here are my mother and brothers! Whoever does God's will is my brother and sister and mother." In Matthew's gospel we read "He replied to him. Pointing to his disciples, he said, 'Here are my mother and my brothers. For whosoever does the will of my Father in heaven is my Brother and sister and brother."

To help us understand what Jesus meant when he said these words, we must keep in mind the whole context of this incident. Jesus was under great pressure, he had been accused of being demonised, even His family thought he was crazy, so he must have been feeling lonely and vulnerable.

The crowds around Jesus would have believed because of the importance of the family in that culture that family members have priority relationship over others. But instead of dropping everything and going out to see his family, Jesus gives a response that would have shocked all that were listening.

There are a number of lessons we need to learn from Jesus' response.

1. Jesus draws a contrast between his natural family and his Spiritual family. There are two ways we can relate to Jesus, we either relate to him naturally or spiritually. When we relate to Jesus at a natural level, we will be like his family, we will try and control him, we will expect him to come to us, our communication with him will be all about us trying to control and manipulate him. On the other hand, to relate to Jesus spiritually is to have a submissive heart that bows to the will of his Father.

2. Enjoying God as father and doing his will increases a persons capacity to relate to Jesus. We cannot live in devotion to the Father and not have intimacy with Jesus.

3.The family of God is made up of those who live under the same authority as Jesus Christ. We cannot be committed to Jesus until we are as committed as Jesus is.

4.Jesus finds his fellowship and acceptance with those who do the will of his father. At this vulnerable time in Jesus' life, he needed those who would understand and accept him. Only those who have a heart set on doing the Fathers will can accept Jesus and what he stands for.

As Jesus pointed out to those seated around him, He was expressing his hearts desire for real intimacy with those who are called disciples. The attitude of Jesus' family towards him at this time should cause us to ask some very important questions.

Why do people try to control others?

What does the controls of others look like? Mark 3:20-21 has a lot to teach us about how as people, we can fall into the trap of trying to control others, or even try to control God. When we set out to control others it is always a deliberate wilful choice we make. It is never a spontaneous thing; it is not the result of a character defect. But the result of deliberate responsible choice. Jesus’ family "went to take charge of him."

Prayer: Prayer, Father help me understand and accept the members of my family as they are, even when they do and think differently to me.
Bible in a year reading plan: -1Samuel 14:43-17:25
